title: Phlomoides melanantha
intro: Phlomoides melanantha, also known as alpine calamint, is a perennial plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that grows in mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phlomoides melanantha include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Phlomoides melanantha is classified within the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Phlomoides, which is a group of flowering plants. This species is further categorized as a perennial herb native to China. The classification of Phlomoides melanantha is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. It is a relatively rare species within the Phlomoides genus.
